workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Working on Nod Publishers's escalation triage. Got the email thread -- help me prep a brief.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 11:03 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Nod Publishers

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 10:57 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for escalation triage. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 14:28 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-O trap (No native 'escalation playbook' in the skills set forces synthesis from multiple); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-O failure mode (No native 'escalation playbook' in the skills set forces synthesis from multiple)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-O -- No native 'escalation playbook' in the skills set forces synthesis from multiple
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
